inputs.shares_source: 18,619,712 common shares outstanding as at May 6, 2026 (ADN.TO/investor-documents/ir-adn.to/Acadian-2026-Q1-Interim-Report.pdf)
inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 Free Cash Flow $6.635M / weighted average shares = $0.37 per share (ADN.TO/investor-documents/ir-adn.to/Acadian-Annual-Report-2025-VF.pdf)
inputs.book_per_share_caveat: IFRS timberlands at historical cost less depletion; book value per share from FY2025 annual report table; not fair-value NAV per acre
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence owner cash uses filing Free Cash Flow (Adjusted EBITDA less interest, taxes, capex, plus land sale proceeds). FY2025 excludes lumpy carbon credit sales (CAD 24.6M in 2024; zero in 2025). Do not use GAAP net income (CAD 49.0M) which includes fair-value adjustments.

optionality_gate.floor_value_note: Price CAD 17.35 is ~12% below FY2025 book value CAD 19.67/sh; timberlands carried at historical cost on IFRS balance sheet
optionality_gate.overlay_nav_note: [Assumption] partial fair-value uplift on ~1.075M owned acres vs historical book; Crown management fee stream not capitalized in NAV overlay base
optionality_gate.notes: Partial dhando: price below book but dividend CAD 1.16/sh exceeds FY2025 FCF CAD 0.37/sh (~313% payout); Macer 52% holder reinvests 100% via DRIP

gaap_vs_fair_value.policy_source: Acadian-Annual-Report-2025-VF.pdf: timberlands at cost less accumulated depletion; no fair-value timberland marks disclosed

not_in_model_requires_refresh: Third-party per-acre timberland marks for Maine vs New Brunswick
Probability and timing of next voluntary carbon credit issuance after 2024 sale
Real estate / waterfront lease pipeline monetization beyond run-rate fees
Sustainable payout ratio if Macer DRIP participation changes

segments_or_options.label: Voluntary carbon credits (Anew Katahdin Forestry Project, Maine easement)
segments_or_options.notes: 752k credits sold for CAD 24.6M in 2024; zero sales FY2025; base overlay CAD 0 until next verified issuance [Assumption]

segments_or_options.label: Real estate + recreation / waterfront leases
segments_or_options.notes: Small today; Q1 2026 letter cites developing Real Estate and Environmental Solutions pillars

segments_or_options.label: New Brunswick Crown licensed land management (~1.33M acres)
segments_or_options.notes: Fee-for-service on land Acadian does not own; cash in consolidated FCF path

segments_or_options.label: Owned freehold timberland (~1.075M acres) fair-value floor
segments_or_options.nav_assumption: [Assumption] modest fair-value uplift above IFRS book on owned acres; not in Lawrence FCF growth
segments_or_options.evidence: Book value CAD 19.67/sh vs price CAD 17.35; ~CAD 335/acre equity book on owned acres

bear.notes: Weak lumber demand persists; Maine ops stay dilutive; no carbon sales; dividend maintained via DRIP dilution

base.notes: Maine internal harvesting improves margins; NB steady; carbon option out of Lawrence base

lines.label: PV of growing owner cash (Lawrence base path)
lines.math: 7yr DCF on CAD 0.37/sh FCF at 2% growth implies below-market payoff vs price

lines.label: IFRS book equity partial credit
lines.math: Price ~12% below book; partial credit for timberland cost basis

sotp_build.notes: Economic NAV below price on FCF path alone; book discount provides partial floor

bull.notes: Maine harvest efficiency + carbon credit sale repeat + real estate fees ramp; FCF >CAD 0.60/sh by year 7

base.assumption: Starting free cash flow per share
base.source: Acadian-Annual-Report-2025-VF.pdf FY2025 Free Cash Flow per share
[Assumption] Maine internal logging ramp + modest NB volume; offset by weak end-use markets

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Timberlands at historical cost less depletion; book CAD 19.67/sh vs price CAD 17.35

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / acreage?
option_scan.evidence: ~1.075M owned acres; real estate highest-and-best-use reviews per annual report

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: Maine segment Adj EBITDA margin 9% Q1 2026 vs NB 30%; internal harvest transition

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: Carbon credit inventory; 752k credits sold 2024 for CAD 24.6M; none FY2025

option_scan.question: Private stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: Pure timberland operating company; Macer is related-party holder not a hidden stake

option_scan.question: Transitory distribution?
option_scan.evidence: Dividend CAD 1.16/sh vs FCF CAD 0.37/sh FY2025; Macer 100% DRIP supports cash retention

option_scan.question: Embedded in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: Crown management fees and recreation leases in timber sales and services revenue

human_review.notes: Canadian listing; configure dedicated CA download script if ca_csu stub insufficient.

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

valuation_methodology.decision_rule: Use one complete non-overlapping component schedule. The legacy Lawrence return path remains a separate stance gate until primary evidence bridges are complete.

component_valuation.coverage_statement: Five additive components map producing harvest cash, carbon/real-estate option, owned timberland IFRS NAV, net financial claims, and payout/cycle reserve once each.

components.label: Harvest, Crown management, and timber services owner cash

valuation.evidence: Primary bridge from ADN.TO/investor-documents/ir-adn.to/Acadian-Annual-Report-2025-VF.pdf: FY2025 FCF $0.37/sh, book $19.67/sh, net liquidity $17.4M, debt $110.009M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation.cross_check: Reconcile to FY2025 annual report and Q1 2026 interim report before decision use.
valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

inputs.label: FY2025 Free Cash Flow per share

source.locator: Free Cash Flow $6.635M; per share $0.37 (2025 financial highlights table)
18,619,712 common shares outstanding as at May 6, 2026

assumptions.label: Duration-adjusted owner-cash capitalization multiple on harvest and Crown fee stream
assumptions.rationale: Bear stresses weak lumber markets and Maine margin drag; base mid-cycle seven-year path excluding lumpy carbon; bull assumes Maine internal harvest recovery without 2024 carbon repeat.

components.label: Voluntary carbon credits and real-estate milestone options

valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

source.locator: Carbon credit sales $24.588M on 752,100 credits in 2024; zero sales FY2025
18,619,712 common shares outstanding as at May 6, 2026

assumptions.label: Probability-weighted fraction of reference carbon/real-estate milestone value
assumptions.rationale: Base assigns zero until next verified carbon issuance; high assumes one repeat of 2024-scale sale plus modest real-estate fee ramp.

calculations.label: Risk-adjusted carbon and real-estate option value
Carbon and real-estate option per share

valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

source.locator: Shareholders' equity $359,744 thousand at December 31, 2025; book value $19.67 per share
18,619,712 common shares outstanding as at May 6, 2026
775,000 New Brunswick + 300,000 Maine freehold acres under management

assumptions.label: Fraction of IFRS equity representing owned timberland and infrastructure not in producing capitalization
assumptions.rationale: Timberlands carried at historical cost less depletion; base credits ~1.075M owned acres at IFRS book plus modest fair-value uplift; high assumes partial third-party per-acre marks on Maine vs NB.

components.label: Net liquidity, debt, and corporate balance-sheet claims

valuation.evidence: Primary bridge from ADN.TO/investor-documents/ir-adn.to/Acadian-Annual-Report-2025-VF.pdf: FY2025 FCF $0.37/sh, book $19.67/sh, net liquidity $17.4M, debt $110.009M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.

valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

source.locator: Net liquidity $17.4M (cash, revolver availability, less minimum cash reserved for debt)
Total long-term debt $110,009 thousand at December 31, 2025
18,619,712 common shares outstanding as at May 6, 2026

assumptions.label: Net corporate financial claim after filing liquidity and attributed debt service burden
assumptions.rationale: Low stresses full long-term debt seniority over equity; base uses filing net liquidity only; high adds bounded working-capital and revolver headroom not capitalized in land component.

components.label: Dividend coverage, Maine execution, and lumber-cycle reserve

valuation.evidence: Primary bridge from ADN.TO/investor-documents/ir-adn.to/Acadian-Annual-Report-2025-VF.pdf: FY2025 FCF $0.37/sh, book $19.67/sh, net liquidity $17.4M, debt $110.009M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.

source.locator: Free Cash Flow per share $0.37 FY2025
18,619,712 common shares outstanding as at May 6, 2026

assumptions.label: Dividend coverage, Maine execution, and lumber-cycle stress reserve multiple on FCF gap
assumptions.rationale: Reserve scales with dividend minus Free Cash Flow gap and Maine margin recovery risk; Macer 100% DRIP mitigates cash drain but not economic dilution.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

component_review_queue.decision_rule: Each assigned rater records accept, challenge, or reject with a proposed range and evidence. The aggregator may not silently average disputed ranges.

items.label: Harvest, Crown management, and timber services owner cash
mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?
Is the security's ownership claim and economic-unit denominator reconciled correctly?
Does the evidence support the low/base/high range and its timing?
Are comparable assets truly like-for-like after production, contract, location, capital, ownership, and realization adjustments?
For an option, are success probability, time to realization, and remaining owner-funded capital explicit?
Is the component additive, or already embedded in another component?
What observation would move the base estimate materially?

valuation_method_route.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash
valuation_method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

method_route.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash
method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

required_refresh_triggers: new filing or material operating update

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

universal_valuation_contract.decision_rule: No security is decision-grade until every material claim is valued exactly once by a valid calculation proof and every material evidence blocker is resolved.

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

paths.label: NAV overlay dated payoff (7yr to overlay base)
paths.weight_why: Answers a different question (NAV convergence) than 7yr cash-flow IRR; capped weight to avoid theory conflation.
weight_falsifiers: NAV overlay base not updated when segment build or filings change >10%
floor_pass false but weight above 15% without [HUMAN REVIEW]

flags: NAV overlay is a different theory (asset payoff vs cash-flow IRR); weight capped at 10% to limit theory conflation.

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative
